summary: Defined canonical operator workflows for all content types, documented Calendly usage workflow with curated context fields, specified normalization behavior, and prepared guide update recommendations.

section_1_role.title: ## 1. Content Library Role
section_1_role.content:
The Content Library is the **first port of call for shareable assets**—links, articles, snippets, and resources that V shares in emails, follow-ups, intros, and communications.

**What it IS:**
- Repository of things to share (Calendly links, articles, bio snippets, decks, trial codes)
- Queryable index for communication workflows (email composer, follow-up generation)
- Normalized storage for web-saved content

**What it is NOT:**
- A routing brain or decision engine
- A CRM or contact database
- A to-do or task tracker

4.1_add_link.title: ### 4.1 Add a Link (Structured, Minimal Content)
4.1_add_link.when: Adding a URL resource (tool, product, app, reference page) that doesn't need full article preservation.
workflow: 3. Add H1 title and brief 'about' section (50-200 chars)
expected_artifacts: DB record with `content_type=link`, `source_file_path` pointing to file
4.1_add_link.example:
```yaml
---
created: 2026-01-18
last_edited: 2026-01-18
version: 1.0
provenance: con_abc123
type: link
subtype: tool
url: https://calendly.com
---

# Calendly

**URL:** https://calendly.com

## About
Scheduling tool for booking meetings. Used for all V's external scheduling.
```

4.2_add_article.title: ### 4.2 Add an Article (Full Content Preservation)
4.2_add_article.when: Saving a substantive article, blog post, newsletter, or essay for future reference or sharing.
workflow: 2. Auto-ingest rule triggers automatically, OR manually run:
- Looks for companion HTML file (saved by `save_webpage`)
- Uses trafilatura to extract clean article text
- Removes navigation, footer, subscribe boxes, ads
4. File moves to `articles/` with clean content + summary
4.2_add_article.fallback:
If trafilatura fails (JS-rendered pages), uses heuristic markdown stripping:
- Removes 'Skip to content', 'Navigation Menu', '© YYYY' patterns
- Removes 'Subscribe', 'Share', 'Related articles' sections

4.3_add_profile_resource.title: ### 4.3 Add a Profile / Resource / Reference
4.3_add_profile_resource.when: Adding structured information about a person, company, GitHub repo, or reference material.
workflow: 1. Create markdown file at appropriate location:
3. Add structured 'About' section with key facts
4.3_add_profile_resource.profile_structure:
For profiles, include:
- Name
- Role/Title
- Company
- Key expertise areas
- LinkedIn URL (if available)
- Brief 1-2 sentence bio
4.3_add_profile_resource.resource_structure:
For resources (GitHub, docs):
- Project name
- What it does (1-2 sentences)
- Key features (bullet list)
- Use cases

4.4_add_media.title: ### 4.4 Add Media (Audio/Video/Deck)
4.4_add_media.when: Storing media files with searchable metadata.
workflow: 1. Place media file in appropriate folder:
4.4_add_media.note: The DB indexes the companion markdown. The media file is accessed via `media_file` field.

content.overview:
Calendly links are stored as `subtype: scheduling-link` under `type: link`. Each active event type has:
- A markdown file at `Knowledge/content-library/links/calendly/<slug>.md`
- A DB record with `content_type=link` and tags including `scheduling-link`

curated_context_fields.description: These fields provide usage context and are **curated** (never overwritten by automation if already set):

priority.purpose: Relative importance when multiple links match
priority.note: High priority links surface first in recommendations

use_when.purpose: Contextual guidance for when to share this link
examples: use_when: General scheduling for new contacts
use_when: Existing clients who need extended time
use_when: Product feedback sessions with beta users

merge_safe_behavior.principle: Automation fills defaults; humans curate context. Existing values are NEVER overwritten.
implementation: 3. On sync: If `audience` is already set in file/DB, sync preserves it
merge_safe_behavior.example_scenario:
```
# File has: audience: investors
# Sync infers: audience: professional (from 'investor' in name)
# Result: audience: investors (original preserved)
```

workflow_add_context.title: Adding Curated Context to Calendly Links
steps: use_when: Schedule with enterprise prospects evaluating Careerspan
3. Save file (no need to re-ingest; sync preserves these)
4. Optionally verify DB: `python3 N5/scripts/content_library.py get calendly_<slug>`

content.sync_command:
```bash
# Full sync (files + DB)
python3 Integrations/calendly/sync_links.py

# Preview changes without writing
python3 Integrations/calendly/sync_links.py --dry-run

# DB-only update (skip file generation)
python3 Integrations/calendly/sync_links.py --db-only
```

what_is_clean_original_text.title: ### 6.1 What 'Clean Original Text' Means
what_is_clean_original_text.definition: Clean original text is the **substantive content** of a webpage with all navigation, chrome, and boilerplate removed.

when_summary_generated.title: ### 6.2 When Summary is Generated
rules: **Fallback:** Use meta description from HTML if available (cheaper than LLM)
**Cached:** Once generated, summary stored in frontmatter and not regenerated
when_summary_generated.summary_format: 50-150 words capturing: main thesis, key points, relevance/value

re_normalize_existing.title: ### 6.3 Re-Normalizing Existing Files
re_normalize_existing.note: v5 normalization only applies to NEW ingests by default.
re_normalize_existing.command:
```bash
# Re-normalize an existing file (when implemented)
python3 N5/scripts/content_ingest.py <file_path> --normalize --force

# The --force flag overwrites existing content with re-normalized version
# Without --force, already-ingested files are skipped
```
re_normalize_existing.caution: Re-normalization may lose manually added content. Consider backup first.

extraction_strategy.title: ### 6.4 Extraction Strategy

primary.quality: Excellent boilerplate removal, handles most sites

fallback.when: No HTML available or trafilatura fails (JS-rendered pages)
patterns: Remove lines matching: 'Skip to content', 'Navigation Menu', 'Toggle navigation'
Remove lines matching: 'Sign in', 'Sign up', 'Log in'
Remove footer patterns: '© 20XX', 'Terms of Service', 'Privacy Policy'
Remove: 'Related articles', 'Read more', 'You might also like'

extraction_strategy.social_exception: X/Twitter and LinkedIn posts: preserve original markdown with light stripping only (post content IS the value)

notes_for_orchestrator: IMPLEMENTATION GAP: W1.3 was design-only. The normalization pipeline (trafilatura, heuristic stripping, summary generation) is NOT yet implemented in content_ingest.py. Documentation describes target behavior.
AUTO-INGEST RULE: Current user rule still has '--type article'. Should be updated to remove explicit type (or update to '--move' only) once v5 auto-detection is implemented.
CALENDLY CURATED FIELDS: Files in links/calendly/ currently lack audience/priority/use_when fields. V should curate these manually; the doc explains how.
GUIDE UPDATE: I've provided the change list but NOT applied it to the guide file (per worker protocol: no commits). Orchestrator or Librarian should apply these changes.
DEPENDENCY: Normalization documentation assumes W1.3 pipeline will be implemented. If implementation is deferred, add 'PLANNED' callout to normalization section.
RECOMMENDATION: Add --normalize flag to content_ingest.py as default-on when implementing W1.3 design.
